---
name: nezam-content-modeling
description: Design content types, field schemas, reusable blocks, and preview/revision workflows for headless CMSes.
version: 1.0.0
updated: 2026-05-08
changelog: []
---
# Purpose

Define a portable, validated content model — types, fields, references, blocks — that decouples content from presentation and supports preview/revision. Single-responsibility: content schema contract.

# Inputs

- IA + taxonomy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-ia-taxonomy/SKILL.md`.
- Page archetype map from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-structured-data-schema/SKILL.md`.
- Editorial role definitions from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-editorial-workflows/SKILL.md`.
- CMS target (Contentful, Sanity, Strapi, Payload, etc.) — from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cms-integration/SKILL.md`.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. List page archetypes (Article, Product, LandingPage, CaseStudy, Author, Tag) and shared blocks (Hero, RichText, MediaGrid, Quote, FAQ, CTA).
2. Define fields per type: name, type (text, RT, slug, image, ref), required, validation, localization.
3. Establish reference rules: which types reference which (Article→Author, Product→Category).
4. Add reusable block library so editors compose pages from known modules instead of free HTML.
5. Define preview, draft, scheduled-publish, and revision history requirements.
6. Document localization strategy: field-level vs entry-level translations, fallback rules.
7. Export schema as portable JSON/YAML and CMS-specific migration files.

# Validation & Metrics

- Every type has explicit required-field validation and slug uniqueness checks.
- Block library covers 90%+ of expected page compositions.
- Preview pipeline returns rendered output for unpublished entries.
- Revision history retains ≥ 30 days or ≥ 20 versions per entry.

# Output Format

- `docs/specs/CONTENT_MODEL.md` (types, fields, references, blocks).
- Portable schema export (`schema/content-model.json`).
- CMS migration stubs (per chosen vendor).
- Localization strategy memo.

# Integration Hooks

- `/PLAN content` produces the model.
- `/DEVELOP` uses migration stubs (without writing app code here).
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cms-integration/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-editorial-workflows/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-ia-taxonomy/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/sdd-design.mdc](.cursor/rules/sdd-design.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- Free-form HTML/Markdown fields that bypass the block library.
- Coupling fields to a specific frontend layout (model leaks UI).
- Skipping validation rules in CMS (allows broken content).
- Ignoring localization until post-launch.
- Single "Page" type with conditional fields instead of typed archetypes.

# External Reference

- Sanity GROQ + schema docs (current).
- Contentful Content Modelling guide (current).
- Strapi Content-Type Builder docs (current).
- "Designing Connected Content" (Carrie Hane, Mike Atherton) for modeling discipline.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: content-modeling.
